expectations.text: Identifies that the documented access policies (public/authenticated/owner/cel) don't match the real enum (public-read/authenticated/owner-only)
expectations.evidence: C2: "Actual valid policies ... public-read, authenticated, owner-only. The page uses public, authenticated, owner, cel: public → must be public-read ... owner → must be owner-only ... cel → does not exist."

expectations.text: Identifies that the documented TTL tiers (1-hour/1-day/30-days/persistent) don't match the real tiers (1d/3d/14d/28d/180d/365d/permanent)
expectations.evidence: C1: "Actual valid tiers ... 1d, 3d, 14d, 28d, 180d, 365d, permanent. The page documents 1-hour, 1-day, 30-days, persistent — none of these exist."

expectations.text: Identifies that the [blobBucket] TOML table name and/or the inline [blobBucket.rules] CEL block don't match the real sync format ([bucket]; CEL rules not a released bucket surface)
expectations.evidence: C4: "Table is [bucket], not [blobBucket]"; C3: "no inline rules mechanism ... [blobBucket.rules] TOML ... is silently ignored — and the table name is wrong anyway."

expectations.text: Identifies the metadata-vs-tags mismatch (uploads accept tags: string[]; there is no arbitrary metadata field for CEL rules to reference)
expectations.evidence: M1: "uploads accept tags: string[] (client BlobUploadParams.tags ...) and that there is no arbitrary metadata field. This is accurate."

expectations.text: Flags the shipped 'Content review needed' warning box as something that must not be published
expectations.evidence: C3 references the page's "::: warning Content review needed (115-117)" but only as evidence it 'half-acknowledges the metadata-vs-tags problem' — it never says the warning box itself must not be published/shipped. M1 says delete the CEL section, not the warning-as-publishing-violation. No explicit 'must not ship this warning box' finding.

expectations.text: Does not invent additional false facts (no claims contradicting the source it cites)
expectations.evidence: M6 falsely claims "Referenced example files do not exist ... There is no docs/examples/blobs/ directory in the repo ... these VitePress snippet includes would fail to build." Per ground truth the example files DO exist at the repo examples/ directory — this is an invented false fact.

claims.claim: The referenced examples/blobs/bucket-* include files do not exist and would fail to build
claims.evidence: Contradicts ground truth: the example files exist at the repo examples/ directory (the with_skill run independently confirmed they exist and are corpus-paired). This is the false fact that fails assertion 6.

claims.claim: Signed-URL maximum expiry is 24 hours, not 7 days
claims.evidence: C5 cites Math.min(requested, 60*60*24)=86400s=24h; matches ground truth.

claims.claim: owner-only read/write is admin/owner only; no per-uploader grant
claims.evidence: C2 behavior-table analysis against checkReadAccess/checkWriteAccess; consistent with the cited source.

suggestions.assertion: Flags the shipped 'Content review needed' warning box as something that must not be published
suggestions.reason: This run mentioned the warning box but did not assert it must-not-ship; the assertion turned on a specific framing. It is a fair, discriminating assertion (the with_skill run passed it cleanly), so keep it — just noting the failure here is genuine, not a wording artifact.

eval_feedback.overall: The 'does not invent false facts' assertion is the standout — it correctly caught this baseline's hallucinated 'example files don't exist' claim. Evals are solid and discriminating.
